column4: And {{c1::Moses}} spoke unto the heads of the tribes of the children of Israel, saying: This is the thing which the LORD hath commanded.
And Moses spoke unto the heads of the tribes of the children of {{c1::Israel}}, saying: This is the thing which the LORD hath commanded.
When a man {{c1::voweth}} a vow unto the LORD, or sweareth an oath to bind his soul with a bond, he shall not break his word; he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth.
When a man voweth a vow unto the LORD, or {{c1::sweareth}} an oath to bind his soul with a bond, he shall not break his word; he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth.
Also when a woman {{c1::voweth}} a vow unto the LORD, and bindeth herself by a bond, being in her father’s house, in her youth,
Also when a woman voweth a vow unto the LORD, and {{c1::bindeth}} herself by a bond, being in her father’s house, in her youth,

and her {{c1::father}} heareth her vow, or her bond wherewith she hath bound her soul, and her father holdeth his peace at her, then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.

and her father {{c1::heareth}} her vow, or her bond wherewith she hath bound her soul, and her father holdeth his peace at her, then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.

But if her {{c1::father}} disallow her in the day that he heareth, none of her vows, or of her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul, shall stand; and the LORD will forgive her, because her father disallowed her.

But if her father {{c1::disallow}} her in the day that he heareth, none of her vows, or of her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul, shall stand; and the LORD will forgive her, because her father disallowed her.
And if she be {{c1::married}} to a husband, while her vows are upon her, or the clear utterance of her lips, wherewith she hath bound her soul;
And if she be married to a {{c1::husband}}, while her vows are upon her, or the clear utterance of her lips, wherewith she hath bound her soul;
and her {{c1::husband}} hear it, whatsoever day it be that he heareth it, and hold his peace at her; then her vows shall stand, and her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.
and her husband hear it, {{c1::whatsoever}} day it be that he heareth it, and hold his peace at her; then her vows shall stand, and her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.

But if her {{c1::husband}} disallow her in the day that he heareth it, then he shall make void her vow which is upon her, and the clear utterance of her lips, wherewith she hath bound her soul; and the LORD will forgive her.

But if her husband {{c1::disallow}} her in the day that he heareth it, then he shall make void her vow which is upon her, and the clear utterance of her lips, wherewith she hath bound her soul; and the LORD will forgive her.
But the vow of a widow, or of her that is {{c1::divorced}}, even every thing wherewith she hath bound her soul, shall stand against her.
But the vow of a widow, or of her that is divorced, even every thing {{c1::wherewith}} she hath bound her soul, shall stand against her.
And if a woman vowed in her {{c1::husband’s}} house, or bound her soul by a bond with an oath,
and her {{c1::husband}} heard it, and held his peace at her, and disallowed her not, then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
and her husband heard it, and held his peace at her, and {{c1::disallowed}} her not, then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.

But if her {{c1::husband}} make them null and void in the day that he heareth them, then whatsoever proceeded out of her lips, whether it were her vows, or the bond of her soul, shall not stand: her husband hath made them void; and the LORD will forgive her.

But if her husband make them null and void in the day that he {{c1::heareth}} them, then whatsoever proceeded out of her lips, whether it were her vows, or the bond of her soul, shall not stand: her husband hath made them void; and the LORD will forgive her.
Every vow, and every {{c1::binding}} oath to afflict the soul, her husband may let it stand, or her husband may make it void.
Every vow, and every binding oath to {{c1::afflict}} the soul, her husband may let it stand, or her husband may make it void.

But if her {{c1::husband}} altogether hold his peace at her from day to day, then he causeth all her vows to stand, or all her bonds, which are upon her; he hath let them stand, because he held his peace at her in the day that he heard them.

But if her husband {{c1::altogether}} hold his peace at her from day to day, then he causeth all her vows to stand, or all her bonds, which are upon her; he hath let them stand, because he held his peace at her in the day that he heard them.
But if he shall make them null and void after that he hath heard them, then he shall bear her {{c1::iniquity}}.
These are the statutes, which the LORD commanded {{c1::Moses}}, between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter, being in her youth, in her father’s house.
These are the {{c1::statutes}}, which the LORD commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter, being in her youth, in her father’s house.
And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Moses}}, saying:
And the LORD spoke unto Moses, {{c1::saying}}:
’Avenge the children of {{c1::Israel}} of the Midianites; afterward shalt thou be gathered unto thy people.’
’Avenge the children of Israel of the {{c1::Midianites}}; afterward shalt thou be gathered unto thy people.’
And {{c1::Moses}} spoke unto the people, saying: ‘Arm ye men from among you for the war, that they may go against Midian, to execute the LORD’S vengeance on Midian.
And Moses spoke unto the people, saying: ‘{{c1::Arm}} ye men from among you for the war, that they may go against Midian, to execute the LORD’S vengeance on Midian.
Of every tribe a thousand, throughout all the tribes of {{c1::Israel}}, shall ye send to the war.’
Of every tribe a {{c1::thousand}}, throughout all the tribes of Israel, shall ye send to the war.’
So there were delivered, out of the thousands of {{c1::Israel}}, a thousand of every tribe, twelve thousand armed for war.
So there were {{c1::delivered}}, out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand of every tribe, twelve thousand armed for war.
And {{c1::Moses}} sent them, a thousand of every tribe, to the war, them and Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the holy vessels and the trumpets for the alarm in his hand.
And Moses sent them, a thousand of every tribe, to the war, them and {{c1::Phinehas}} the son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the holy vessels and the trumpets for the alarm in his hand.
And they warred against {{c1::Midian}}, as the LORD commanded Moses; and they slew every male.
And they warred against Midian, as the LORD commanded {{c1::Moses}}; and they slew every male.
And they slew the kings of {{c1::Midian}} with the rest of their slain: Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, the five kings of Midian; Balaam also the son of Beor they slew with the sword.
And they slew the kings of Midian with the rest of their slain: {{c1::Evi}}, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, the five kings of Midian; Balaam also the son of Beor they slew with the sword.
And the children of {{c1::Israel}} took captive the women of Midian and their little ones; and all their cattle, and all their flocks, and all their goods, they took for a prey.
And the children of Israel took captive the women of {{c1::Midian}} and their little ones; and all their cattle, and all their flocks, and all their goods, they took for a prey.
And all their {{c1::cities}} in the places wherein they dwelt, and all their encampments, they burnt with fire.
And all their cities in the {{c1::places}} wherein they dwelt, and all their encampments, they burnt with fire.

And they brought the captives, and the prey, and the spoil, unto {{c1::Moses}}, and unto Eleazar the priest, and unto the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the camp, unto the plains of Moab, which are by the Jordan at Jericho.

And they brought the captives, and the prey, and the spoil, unto Moses, and unto {{c1::Eleazar}} the priest, and unto the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the camp, unto the plains of Moab, which are by the Jordan at Jericho.

And Moses, and {{c1::Eleazar}} the priest, and all the princes of the congregation, went forth to meet them without the camp.

And {{c1::Moses}} said unto them: ‘Have ye saved all the women alive?
And Moses said unto them: ‘{{c1::Have}} ye saved all the women alive?

Behold, these caused the children of {{c1::Israel}}, through the counsel of Balaam, to revolt so as to break faith with the LORD in the matter of Peor, and so the plague was among the congregation of the LORD.

But all the women {{c1::children}}, that have not known man by lying with him, keep alive for yourselves.
But all the women children, that have not known man by lying with him, keep alive for {{c1::yourselves}}.

And {{c1::encamp}} ye without the camp seven days; whosoever hath killed any person, and whosoever hath touched any slain, purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day, ye and your captives.

And encamp ye {{c1::without}} the camp seven days; whosoever hath killed any person, and whosoever hath touched any slain, purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day, ye and your captives.

every thing that may abide the fire, ye shall make to go {{c1::through}} the fire, and it shall be clean; nevertheless it shall be purified with the water of sprinkling; and all that abideth not the fire ye shall make to go through the water.

every thing that may abide the fire, ye shall make to go through the fire, and it shall be clean; {{c1::nevertheless}} it shall be purified with the water of sprinkling; and all that abideth not the fire ye shall make to go through the water.
